summary: Fetches RSS/Atom feeds with HTTP conditional request support (ETag/Last-Modified) and a compound checkpoint encoding timestamp, ETag, and Last-Modified

requires: Checkpoint format: '<RFC3339>|<etag>|<last_modified>' (pipe-delimited; etag and last_modified may be empty)
guarantees: On 304 Not Modified, returns empty messages with unchanged checkpoint
Checkpoint always encodes the newest item timestamp seen; ETag/Last-Modified are appended when provided by server
Item order follows feed order (not sorted by this code)

effects: HTTP GET to feedURL with optional If-None-Match / If-Modified-Since headers

observations.text: The checkpoint is a compound string '<RFC3339>|<etag>|<last_modified>'. This is the most sophisticated checkpoint format in the source system — it bundles both content-based (timestamp) and HTTP-based (conditional headers) deduplication into one opaque string.

itemTimestamp falls back to time.Now() when both PublishedParsed and UpdatedParsed are nil. This means undated items are treated as 'just published', causing them to always pass the checkpoint filter and be returned on every call.
Text composition priority: Content (full text/HTML) > Description (summary) > Title. Items may contain raw HTML if the feed uses content:encoded — no sanitization performed.
Uses mmcdole/gofeed library to parse both RSS 2.0 and Atom feeds through a single unified API, abstracting format differences.
When newest timestamp is zero (empty feed after filtering), checkpoint falls back to the input checkpoint string unchanged. This prevents checkpoint regression.
Uses http.DefaultClient with no timeout — consistent risk with HNSource and RedditSource.
